name: coupling_fep_igt

pytorch.reason: gradient descent on FEP free energy and gradient ascent on IGT expected payoff; both converge to same Nash fixed point when generative model encodes Nash payoffs

pyg.reason: not needed; no GNN in this sim

z3.reason: UNSAT: FEP fixed point (F=KL=0) requires q=p; if p encodes Nash payoffs then q=Nash; cannot have FEP equilibrium and off-Nash simultaneously

cvc5.reason: not needed; z3 handles FEP-Nash equivalence proof

sympy.reason: symbolic differentiation: dF/dq=0 iff q=p; dE[u]/dsigma=0 at Nash; proves both gradient conditions coincide when p encodes Nash payoff distribution

clifford.reason: grade-1 belief vector in Cl(3,0); Nash fixed point as grade-0 KL=0 scalar; proves FEP dynamics are grade-reduction toward Nash invariant

geomstats.reason: not needed; Clifford grade-reduction handles belief projection

e3nn.reason: not needed; no equivariant network in this sim

rustworkx.reason: FEP update DAG and IGT best-response DAG: both sink at Nash equilibrium node (0.5, 0.5); proves Nash is the common attractor for both gradient dynamics

xgi.reason: active inference as triadic hyperedge {fep_agent, igt_opponent, outcome}; proves FEP-IGT coupling is irreducibly higher-order, not decomposable into pairs

toponetx.reason: not needed; graph sink analysis done by rustworkx

gudhi.reason: not needed; no persistent homology in this sim

pytorch_fep_igt_converge_same_fixed_poin.claim: FEP agent (minimize KL) and IGT agent (maximize E[u]) both converge to sigma*=(0.5, 0.5) Nash equilibrium when p encodes Nash payoff distribution; same fixed point: F-min IS Nash equilibrium

sympy_fep_igt_stationary_conditions_equi.claim: dF/dq=0 at q=p (FEP stationary); dE[u]/dsigma=0 at Nash (IGT stationary); when p encodes Nash payoffs, q*=p=sigma* and both conditions are identical

clifford_belief_grade1_nash_grade0.claim: FEP belief state is grade-1 vector in Cl(3,0); Nash equilibrium is the grade-0 scalar fixed point (KL=0); FEP dynamics reduce grade-1 belief to its grade-0 invariant (Nash value)

rustworkx_fep_igt_share_nash_sink.claim: FEP belief-update DAG and IGT best-response DAG both have (0.5, 0.5) as their unique sink node; Nash equilibrium is the common attractor of both dynamics

xgi_active_inference_triadic_hyperedge.claim: Active inference (FEP in game context) is triadic {agent, opponent, outcome}; cannot be decomposed into bilateral FEP-perception and IGT-response separately; higher-order hyperedge captures the agent-game coupling

z3_fep_fixed_point_and_off_nash_unsat.claim: UNSAT: FEP fixed point (F=0, q=p_nash) AND off-Nash (q != p_nash) simultaneously; if p encodes Nash payoffs, FEP minimum IS Nash; cannot minimize F while being off-Nash

pytorch_wrong_generative_model_misses_na.claim: FEP with wrong generative model (p=0.8/0.2) converges to p_wrong, NOT Nash (0.5/0.5); FEP-Nash equivalence requires p to encode Nash payoffs; wrong model breaks the coupling

sympy_kl_positive_off_nash.claim: KL(q||p_nash) > 0 when q != p_nash; FEP agent off-Nash has positive free energy; F=0 requires q=p_nash; confirms FEP is NOT at Nash when beliefs are wrong

pytorch_zero_sum_mixed_nash_fep.claim: Zero-sum game (matching pennies): FEP with p=(0.5, 0.5) converges to mixed Nash (0.5, 0.5); FEP-Nash equivalence holds for mixed equilibria too

clifford_nash_belief_non_degenerate_cont.claim: At Nash belief (0.5*e1 + 0.5*e2), outer product with any non-collinear opponent is non-degenerate; Nash equilibrium is a stable grade-2 contract point

rustworkx_combined_convergence_same_sink.claim: Combined FEP+IGT convergence graph: both dynamics reach Nash sink; boundary case: starting from same belief state, convergence paths overlap

xgi_solo_fep_degenerate_no_game.claim: FEP agent with no game partner: degenerate hyperedge (size 1); FEP-IGT coupling requires at least one opponent; solo FEP is not IGT-coupled

coupling_claim: FEP (free energy minimization, active inference) and IGT (Nash equilibrium, IESDS) are structurally coupled: both are gradient descents on different functionals (F = KL(q||p) for FEP; E[u|sigma] for IGT) that converge to the same fixed point when the generative model p encodes Nash payoffs. UNSAT: FEP fixed point (F=0, q=p_nash) AND off-Nash simultaneously is impossible. Sympy: dF/dq=0 at q=p and dE[u]/dsigma=0 at Nash are equivalent when p encodes Nash. Active inference in game context is irreducibly triadic {agent, opponent, outcome}.
